results.title: They found a cave; Waxworks (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Andrew Steane, Charles E. Wolnizer and Australian Council for Children's Film and Television
results.brief_description:
They found a cave: Four English orphans migrate to Tasmania under the care of their Aunt. Their Aunt becomes sick and is hospitalised, leaving the children to seek refuge in a secret cave. Excitement follows the children as they face danger and adventure in the rugged terrain before they are finally reunited with their Aunt. Based on the novel by Nan Chauncy.

Waxworks: A short tale about children lost in a waxworks museum “House of Horrors”.

results.title: Xos: a cry for help
results.creator_credit: Alan Woodruff, Roberta Chuter and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description:
Famous drag queen Holly and her scheming sister Beryl (both played by Doug Lucas) battle over the wishes of their recently deceased grandmother who wants to be buried with her canine companion, Dingo. Defying all health codes and decorum, Holly and her long-suffering man servant hatch a plan to fulfill her grandmother’s wishes, while Beryl plots a much darker fate for her deluded sister.

Alan Woodruff’s student film is a droll drag curio that gives its lead actor a wonderful showcase to play up and act out in two roles no less. Referencing the likes of Sunset Boulevard, Prisoner and larger than life romance novelist Barbra Cartland, ‘XOS: A Cry for Help’ is an unexpected camp delight.

results.title: Fruit fly
results.creator_credit: Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: The Mediterranean fruit fly is a serious pest of the fruit industry in Western Australia. It infests so many different types of fruit that it keeps alive throughout the seasons. It is the larvae that do the damage. It is necessary to keep orchards absolutely clean. One dirty orchard can affect the whole district.

results.title: The amateurist
results.material_description: Single channel moving image, colour and audio
results.brief_description: A short, captivating video about surveillance, identity, watching and being watched. ‘The Amateurist’ slides along the edges of horror and satire to create an unsettling portrait of a woman on the brink of a technologically-driven madness.

results.title: Breeding for quality
results.creator_credit: Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: England, Denmark, New Zealand and America are the major dairying countries of the world which use artificial insemination. In order to improve the dairy herds in Western Australia the government has set up a research station which supplies commercial herds with semen from top-quality bulls. Records show that artificial insemination has proved its worth to the dairy farmer in increased production, better quality milk and healthier herds.

results.title: Roundabout
results.creator_credit: Rachel Griffiths, Jason Byrne, Louise Smith, 3rd Pictures and Igloo Films
results.brief_description: A stressed businessman experiences a panic attack after he hits a dog in his car on his way to work. Spiralling out of control he drives in endless circles, unable to stop. Photography, Tristan Milani ; music, David Bridie ; editing, Geordie Anderson. Cast: David Roberts, Alison Whyte, Kym Gyngell.

results.title: The Mirror of man
results.brief_description: A statement on behalf of contemporary art. Presents the movement from the first break with classical form to our art expression of today. Paintings used in the film were selected from the 1958 Retrospective Exhibition at the Carnegie Institute at Pittsburgh, which presented a survey of paintings shown at the International Exhibitions of the Institute since 1894. Commentator: Vincent Price

results.title: Bachdisc
results.brief_description: The Bachdisc marks a milestone in the treatment of musical subjects in laserdisc format. How does one add visually to something that is not only heard, but is considered to be the most subjective of the arts? Bachdisc creator, Juan Downey, incorporates onto a multiple-image screen various images of the famous New York harpsichordist Elaine Comparone playing Bach’s Fugue #24 in B minor. On side 2 of the disc is a documentary of Bach’s personal life and work.

results.title: Pauses
results.creator_credit: Monique Schwarz, International Women's Year Committee and Melbourne State College
results.brief_description: Examines the lives of three successful women who are in their forties. Joyce Nicholson, a writer and business woman, Helen Laycock, a potter and Rosie Smith who runs a craft business. Each describe their work and their experiences as a wife and mother. A useful film discussion starter about woman’s role in Australian society.
